title: The semis entry-signal model
question: Can a confirmation-stack checklist actually time entries into the semis complex - long and short - or is it just a tidy way to stay disciplined?
finding: It is a disciplined checklist, not a fitted alpha, and the honest spine is much smaller than the pitch. The daily long-entry stack (uptrend + volatility compression + volume-confirmed breakout + blow-off filter) fired only 11 times in ten years (2016-05-18 to 2026-06-08): fwd20 mean +7.55%, median +6.51%, hit 72.7%, signal Sharpe 0.49 vs buy-and-hold 0.27, deflated Sharpe 0.9383 - the DSR clears the 0.60 bar but the grade is WEAK only because n=11 fails the n>=20 EDGE rule, and the setup mean (+7.55%) barely beats the buy-and-hold mean (+7.21%), so the edge is lower entry volatility, not higher return. The short side, expressed by buying the 3x inverse ETF on a breakdown setup, actively LOSES (n=142: fwd20 mean -16.65%, median -15.57%, hit 16.9%, max drawdown -100%, deflated Sharpe 0.0) - and the loss is monotone with horizon, the decay signature of the inverse-leveraged vehicle, not a timing miss. Two supporting factors each grade EDGE on raw IC but under load-bearing caveats. Options positioning (17 SOXX holdings, 563 EOD option-days, 2024-03-11 to 2026-06-09) is the strongest single factor: Spearman IC vs forward SOXX of put/call +0.389 and OI-weighted IV -0.467 at fwd20 (best absolute IC 0.467), contrarian-signed (crowd fear precedes bounce; expensive vol marks tops), but it is fitted to a one-sided bull, EOD positioning ~8 days stale at the live edge, and 20-day windows overlap so the effective sample is ~25 not ~490 (de-overlapped t falls to ~2.0 for put/call and ~2.5 for IV). Social attention (912 stance-free posts, ~5 months, 95 overlapping obs) leads the next week (attention IC +0.311, credible-handle +0.305 at fwd5) then decays by fwd20 (attention +0.074) except the credibility-weighted count (+0.272); it is buzz not direction, and on ~19 independent five-day blocks +0.31 is ~1-1.5 SE from zero. All numbers gross; the backtest tests the daily skeleton only - the three live-only gates (regime/size dial, credit confirmation, realtime order-flow) were never captured historically and are excluded. Verdict: Conditional, conditioned on honesty about what it is - keep the checklist as a discipline bolted onto the study 21 risk dial, treat the two factors as context indicators, and do not trade the short side through an inverse ETF.
